# Question 123

*UGC NET CS · 2023 Mar 11 Shift 2 Dec 2022 Session · Context-Free Language · Languages generated by grammars*

Match the following based on the language accepted by using brute force method of parsing. LIST II LIST I A. 5 aSa aa (20 *3) - +. n21 S→aaSaa aa B. II. 20: 021 S→aaa Saaaaa III (4*20)- 6) : n21 D. S-aaaaSaaaa | aa IV. 25 - 2; nz2 Choose the correct answer from the options given below:

- **1.** A-IV. B-I. C-L, D-I1
- **2.** A-II, B- IV, C-I, D-III
- **3.** A-I, B- IV, C-III, D-II
- **4.** A-IL, B-III, C-I, D-IV

> [!TIP]
> **Correct answer: 2. A-II, B- IV, C-I, D-III**

## Solution

For A, base length 2 and each recursion adds 2, giving 2n (II). B adds 4 to base 2, giving 4n−2 (IV). C adds 6, giving 6n−4 (I). D adds 8, giving 8n−6 (III). Thus A–II, B–IV, C–I, D–III.

## Key Points

- For S→uSv|w, generated length is |w|+k(|u|+|v|).

## Why the other options are incorrect

The alternatives mismatch the arithmetic progressions produced by recursive wrapping.
